skill.name: design-orchestration
skill.description: Orchestrates design workflows by routing work through brainstorming, multi-agent review, and execution readiness in the correct order. Prevents premature implementation, skipped validation, and unreviewed high-risk designs.
skill.summary: Orchestrates design workflows by routing work through brainstorming, multi-agent review, and executi...
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported external command and weak cryptography patterns, but review found only markdown prose and backticked skill names. No shell execution, cryptographic code, network access, filesystem access, or prompt injection attempt was found in SKILL.md.

low_findings.title: External Command Static Findings Are Skill References
low_findings.description: The flagged external command locations are markdown references to workflow skills. They do not contain shell syntax, process execution, command arguments, or user-controlled command construction.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The inspected lines contain markdown bullets and inline code spans naming workflow skills. No executable shell syntax, command runner, or user-controlled input path is present.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Static Findings Are Prose
low_findings.description: The flagged weak cryptography locations are skill metadata, headings, or design workflow text. No cryptographic API, hashing function, cipher, key handling, or executable code was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed file is a markdown skill instruction document, not source code. The flagged lines do not call cryptographic primitives or define security-sensitive algorithms.

content.value_statement: Complex product and system changes can move to implementation before the design is clear. This skill routes work through brainstorming, risk review, and readiness checks before execution starts.
actual_capabilities: Routes new feature or system requests to brainstorming before implementation.
Classifies design risk as low, moderate, or high using stated criteria.
Requires multi-agent review for high-risk designs and recommends it for moderate-risk designs.
Checks for approved design artifacts, decision logs, assumptions, and known risks.
Identifies the next workflow step when required design evidence is missing.
limitations: Does not generate designs by itself.
Depends on separate brainstorming and multi-agent brainstorming skills.
Does not execute implementation work or modify project files.
Requires users to provide enough context for risk classification.

use_cases.title: Plan Product Changes
use_cases.description: Guide feature requests through idea development, review, and readiness checks before engineering work begins.

use_cases.title: Review System Designs
use_cases.description: Require additional review for changes with operational risk, high complexity, or uncertain assumptions.

use_cases.title: Control AI Coding Workflows
use_cases.description: Prevent an assistant from starting implementation until design artifacts and decisions are complete.

prompt_templates.title: Start a Design Workflow
prompt_templates.scenario: Begin with a new idea that needs structure.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use design-orchestration for this feature idea. Route me to the correct first step and tell me what artifacts are required.

prompt_templates.title: Assess Design Risk
prompt_templates.scenario: Decide whether a design needs extra review.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use design-orchestration to classify this design as low, moderate, or high risk. Explain the required next step.

prompt_templates.title: Check Review Readiness
prompt_templates.scenario: Confirm whether a design can move toward implementation.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use design-orchestration to verify whether this design has the required approval, decision log, assumptions, and risk notes.

prompt_templates.title: Govern a High-Risk Change
prompt_templates.scenario: Coordinate strict review for a complex or sensitive change.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use design-orchestration for this high-risk change. Enforce the required brainstorming, multi-agent review, and execution readiness sequence.

output_examples.input: I want to redesign our billing flow and start coding today.
output_examples.output: Next step: run brainstorming first. The change affects users and revenue, so implementation should wait for an initial design and decision log.

output_examples.input: The design is approved, but assumptions are not documented.
output_examples.output: Execution is not ready. Document the major assumptions, update the decision log, then repeat the readiness check.

output_examples.input: A reviewed design has final disposition REVISE.
output_examples.output: Next step: return to design revision. Do not proceed to implementation until the revised design receives approval.

best_practices: Provide the current design, decision log, assumptions, and known risks when invoking the skill.
Use multi-agent review for changes with user impact, irreversible effects, or high uncertainty.
Ask for an explicit next step before moving from design work to implementation planning.
anti_patterns: Using the skill as a design generator instead of a workflow router.
Skipping review because a design feels simple without documenting the risk classification.
Starting implementation while approval, assumptions, or decision log entries are missing.

faq.question: Does this skill create the design?
faq.answer: No. It routes the workflow and checks whether the required design steps are complete.

faq.question: When should I use it?
faq.answer: Use it before implementation when a feature, system change, or design decision needs review discipline.

faq.question: Does it require multi-agent review every time?
faq.answer: No. It requires multi-agent review for high-risk designs and recommends it for moderate-risk designs.

faq.question: Can it block implementation?
faq.answer: Yes. It can instruct the workflow to stop when required design artifacts or approvals are missing.

faq.question: Which tools can use it?
faq.answer: The report lists Claude, Codex, and Claude Code as supported tools.

faq.question: Does it run commands or access files?
faq.answer: No evidence found. The skill is a markdown instruction file and does not contain executable command code.
